rule.desc: Define and honor nullness contracts for public APIs.
rule.rationale: Clear nullness avoids NPEs and makes call sites safer.

rule.desc: Use `Optional<T>` for return values that may be absent.
rule.rationale: Optional makes absence explicit and avoids `null` checks.

rule.desc: Prefer primitives over boxed types unless nullability or collections require boxing.

example.code: // Bad - can be null and slower Integer retries = 3;

rule.rationale: Inconsistent equality breaks collections and caching.
Inconsistency causes undefined behavior in sorted collections.

rule.desc: Use records for simple, immutable data carriers (Java 16+).
rule.exceptions: Use classes when you need inheritance or custom serialization behavior.
rule.rationale: Records provide concise, correct value semantics.
